summary:
GAME DAY 1: THE AVALANCHE ROLLS

Well well well, look who's already in the trenches. Zach spends three months organising this whole thing and his South Africa pick gets absolutely battered 2-0 in the opening match 😅 Carole's Mexico meanwhile are out here looking like actual contenders in Tier 1, so that's one person feeling very smug right now. The rest of you lot are still in the waiting room but don't get comfortable because there's a lot of football left to ruin your dreams.

South Africa couldn't score if the goal was indoors, and that's going to cost Zach dearly if this keeps up 🫠 Meanwhile Mexico are already building their highlight reel and frankly it's getting annoying to watch. The Wooden Spoon conversation has officially started on day one, which feels harsh but also completely deserved.

One match in and we've got a hierarchy. Carole's sitting pretty, Zach's wondering why he didn't just donate his entry fee to charity, and the rest of you are hoping your picks don't pull a South Africa 🔥 Welcome to the Avalanche, folks.

The Avalanche - Game Day 2 Update

Well well well, Rory's Czechia just got absolutely flattened by South Korea 🔥 and honestly mate, it could've been worse. Meanwhile Fiona and Lynne are locked in a beautiful stalemate with Canada and Bosnia playing like they've got a handshake agreement to bore everyone senseless. At least they both got a point, which is more than Rory can say. Ian's sitting pretty with South Korea's win, which means Zach's sweepstakes is already paying out to someone other than the organiser 😂 classic move lad.

The group stage is shaping up nicely for chaos and we're only two games deep. Rory's sweating, the draw merchants are celebrating draws like they won the lottery, and Ian's quietly accumulating wins like he knew something we didn't. There's still plenty of football to play but someone's Tier 3 fantasy is already looking like a Tier 3 disaster.

Zach organised this whole thing just to watch his own teams crumble, apparently.

The Avalanche rolls on and the cracks are showing already 😬. Ian's flying high with USA's demolition of Paraguay while poor Paul watched his Tier 2 pick get absolutely battered 4-1, which is genuinely embarrassing. Meanwhile Daire, Derek and Lynne are all sitting pretty with draws from their Tier 1 picks, proving once again that playing it safe is the coward's way to glory. Zach's sitting back watching his masterpiece unfold and honestly we should be worried about whatever chaos he's quietly plotting.

Ana's Tier 3 Qatar draw is holding up better than expected which means the sweepstakes gods are feeling generous today 🎪. The real story though? Paul's Paraguay meltdown has him already eyeing up the Beautiful Loser prize before we've even kicked off properly. At this rate he'll be writing acceptance speeches by next week. Brazil and Morocco's stalemate means Daire and Derek are splitting the neutrals' vote, which is delightfully pointless for both of them.

We're three games in and Ian's already got one foot on the podium ✅. Everyone else is treading water except Paul who's treading something else entirely. See you tomorrow for more chaos.

Right lads, Game Day 4 has served us a proper mixed bag and honestly 🍿 the schadenfreude is delicious. Ronán's Germany absolutely demolished Curaçao 7-1 which is basically cheating at this point, while poor Derek watched his Curaçao pick get absolutely filleted in the same match. Zach organised this whole thing and Turkey's already waving the white flag after losing 0-2 to Australia, which is exactly the energy we needed today 😅

Fiona and Peter are both sitting pretty with their Netherlands T1 pick earning a solid draw against Japan, though that's basically a loss when you're hoping for three points. Carole's Ivory Coast flexed with a 1-0 win over Ecuador which means Daire's watching his T2 pick crumble already. The real winner though? Aidan sweeping both sides of the Scotland-Haiti coin with a clean sheet, which is proper elite sweepstakes management right there.

We're only four days in and the Wooden Spoon is already shaping up to be Derek's unless Germany decides to have mercy on us 💀 Zach, mate, your tournament organisation skills are immaculate but your Turkey pick is giving "I chose this at 11pm on my phone" energy.

Well well well, Game Day 5 and the chaos is *chef's kiss* 🔥. Ana's absolutely flying with Belgium's draw plus Sweden's demolition of Tunisia, while poor Lynne watched her Tier 3 pick get absolutely battered 5-1 (that's what you get for being optimistic about Tunisia). Meanwhile Zach, our glorious organiser, got a stunning 0-0 masterclass from Spain vs Cape Verde Islands — nothing says "I planned this perfectly" like watching your Tier 1 pick bore everyone to death 😅.

The real entertainment though? Gran somehow managed to pick both sides of the Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay snooze fest in different tiers, so at least one of those draws is working for them. Sian's Tier 2 Egypt held firm with a draw, which is basically a moral victory when you're facing Belgium. But let's be honest, Ana's the one laughing right now while the rest of you lot scramble for underdog points.

Still early days but if Zach's masterplan involves watching his own teams flop, he's absolutely nailed it 💀.

The Avalanche strikes again and it's CHAOS out there 🌪️. Mark's absolutely printing money with France absolutely dismantling Senegal while his other picks are doing their thing, which is basically the dream scenario when you're sitting pretty in Tier 1. Meanwhile Daire's Iran decided that 2-2 was a perfectly acceptable outcome for his Tier 3 investment, which is fair play honestly. Paul's meanwhile watching Norway absolutely batter Iraq 4-1 and deciding his organiser buddy Zach probably should've checked these matchups before locking them in 😅.

The real entertainment is that Zach set all this up and his teams are already getting a proper kicking. That's what we call poetic justice, lads. Mark's out here playing 4D chess while the rest of us are still working out the rules 🍺.

THE AVALANCHE — GAME DAY 7 UPDATE

Well, well, well. Sian's absolutely cooking 🔥 with two wins already, while the rest of us are still searching for our second coffee. Argentina's demolition of Algeria was pure theatre, Ghana scraped by Panama, and somehow Aidan managed to pick Portugal of all teams to draw 1-1 — mate, that's called hedging your bets and it shows. Meanwhile Ronán's watching both his picks crumble like digestive biscuits, and Peter's sitting comfortably with Austria's convincing win even if his Jordan gamble evaporated faster than Zach's credibility organizing this whole thing 😂.

England's 4-2 battering of Croatia has Gran grinning ear to ear while Ian's consoling himself that at least they scored. Fiona's Congo DR draw keeps her just barely afloat, but let's be honest, nobody's backing into the Underdog Hero prize on this trajectory. The points are spreading out like butter on hot toast — Sian's already thinking semi-finals while half of us are wondering if we picked actual football teams or just names that sounded vaguely professional.

Zach organized a tournament where his own picks are basically donating to charity. That's what we call poetic justice ⚽.

Right then, Game Day 8 and The Avalanche is properly moving 🏔️ Rory's already cruising with Colombia's demolition job while Lynne absolutely cleaned house, getting Switzerland's 4-1 thrashing to cover both her Tier picks. Meanwhile Fiona's watching Canada embarrass Qatar 6-0 which is absolutely beautiful timing for her Tier 2, and absolutely BRUTAL for Ana who picked Qatar as her Tier 3 wildcard. Six-nil. Ana, mate, that's not a prediction, that's a war crime.

But here's where it gets spicy: our dear organiser Zach has South Africa locked into Tier 3, and they just scraped a 1-1 draw against Czechia. That's not nothing, but it's not exactly the kind of thing that wins sweepstakes 😬 Meanwhile Carole's Uzbekistan got absolutely mugged 1-3, so Tier 3 is already taking casualties left and right.

Zach's out here running the whole thing and his picks are already looking dodgier than a three-quid note.

Game Day 9 and The Avalanche keeps rolling. Carole's Mexico finally showed up with that 1-0 over South Korea, and Derek's got Morocco doing the business in Scotland 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 meanwhile getting absolutely battered by the USA. Ian's sitting pretty with two Tier 1s firing but his South Korea gamble is looking about as sharp as a chocolate teapot. Poor Aidan watched Scotland get done 0-1 and honestly mate, that's on you.

Here's the thing though — Zach organised this entire sweepstakes and his teams are already looking dodgy 😅 which is absolutely brilliant for the rest of us. Derek's taking hits on both sides of the ledger but that Morocco pick might keep him alive. The Tier 3s are starting to sweat and we're only on Game 9, which means beautiful chaos incoming.

Ian's the only one genuinely cooking right now, but let's see how long that lasts before someone's Tier 2 collapses like a house of cards 🃏 The real entertainment starts when the big nations wake up.

Game Day 10 and The Avalanche is properly rolling now. Ronán and Daire both bagged wins with their Tier 1 picks, so they're laughing while the rest of us scramble. Peter's Netherlands absolutely demolished Sweden 5-1 😅 which is brilliant for him but absolutely tragic for Ana who watched her Tier 2 get humiliated on live telly. Zach, mate, your Turkey pick just lost 0-1 to Paraguay and honestly I'm enjoying this more than I should.

The real chaos though is Aidan's Haiti getting absolutely demolished 3-0 by Brazil. Your Tier 3 pick is already leaking goals like a sieve and we're barely through two weeks. Carole's Ivory Coast went down fighting at least, but Zach you're now sweating because both your picks are looking shaky. Paul's Paraguay sneaked a 1-0 win so he's suddenly in the mix too.

Ronán and Peter are the ones to beat right now. Aidan, you're already eyeing that Underdog Hero prize but mate, Haiti's got work to do 💪. The Beautiful Loser and Wooden Spoon races are wide open and honestly, watching Zach's organiser's curse unfold has been worth the entry fee alone.

Right, Game Day 11 and we've got some proper chaos brewing. Fiona absolutely fleece-d Tunisia 4-0 with Japan 🔥 while Zach's Spain did the same to Saudi Arabia, so our organiser's finally got something to smile about instead of just watching his spreadsheet weep. Meanwhile Rory's Cape Verde Islands pulled off the madness with a 2-2 draw against Uruguay, which means his Tier 3 is somehow still breathing when literally nobody saw that coming. On the flip side, poor Lynne watched Tunisia get absolutely demolished and Gran's Saudi Arabia got turned inside out, so the Beautiful Loser race is getting spicy 😅.

The real laugh is Daire somehow coasting with two consecutive draws across both his picks — Ecuador and Iran just said no thanks to scoring today. Derek's Curaçao also joined the blank sheet club. Ana's Belgium came away scoreless too, so we're seeing a right snooze-fest of 0-0s while everyone else is either celebrating bangers or crying into their pints.

Zach organised a sweepstakes where the first thing that happens is his own teams start printing money — absolutely the vibe of someone who definitely didn't rig this.

Game Day 12: The Avalanche Rumbles

Sian's absolutely flying 🏔️ and has cleaned up today with Argentina's clinical demolition and Egypt's three-goal masterclass, meaning she's basically printed money while the rest of us sat here watching our dreams die. Mark got the France party going with that Iraq hammering but New Zealand imploded spectacularly, so it's one win one catastrophe vibes. Meanwhile Paul's Iraq got obliterated 0-3 (mate, we all saw that coming) and Peter's Austria got schooled by Messi and friends 😬, so they're both circling the drain wondering if there's any Tier 3 magic left to salvage this.

The Wooden Spoon is genuinely up for grabs now with Austria, Iraq and New Zealand all bleeding goals like they've never heard of a defensive shape 💀. Zach, your master plan is working perfectly... for literally everyone except possibly you. Let's see if anyone can still make the Beautiful Loser money or if we're all just here for the schadenfreude.

Sian's basically already spending her runner-up money 💶.

GAME DAY 13: THE AVALANCHE ROLLS ON

Paul's Norway absolutely demolished Senegal 3-2 in a proper thriller 🎢, while Aidan kept things clinical with Portugal's brutal 5-0 whitewash over Uzbekistan. Ronán picked up the midweek gift as Algeria scraped past Jordan 2-1, but watch him sweat now because Panama got absolutely mugged off 0-1 to Croatia. Meanwhile, Gran and Sian both took the boring route as England couldn't be bothered scoring against Ghana in a 0-0 masterclass of "let's go home early." Mark and Carole got properly battered with Senegal's loss and that absolutely embarrassing Portugal hammering.

The leaderboard's tightening up nicely with some proper contenders emerging. Paul, Aidan, and Ronán are looking dangerous, but don't sleep on Ian who's quietly picking up winners 🏔️. Peter and Carole are staring down the barrel of some serious prizes though — one of them might be funding the Underdog Hero pot if they keep backing absolute duffers.

Sian's somehow survived another day looking like a tactical genius when really England just couldn't be bothered attacking.

Game Day 14: The Avalanche rolls on and honestly it's getting brutal out there. Rory and Derek both landed clean Tier 1 wins with Colombia and Morocco putting on a show 🔥 but the real moment belongs to Lynne, who's now sitting pretty with a Tier 1 AND Tier 2 double up — Switzerland and Bosnia both coming through when it mattered. Meanwhile Aidan's taking it on the chin with Scotland getting absolutely demolished by Brazil and Haiti serving up a four-goal buffet to Morocco, so his T3 hopes are looking thinner than his excuse game right now.

Daire's grinning like an idiot after Brazil absolutely dismantled Scotland 3-0, which means he's still very much alive in the Tier 1 race. But poor Fiona got the double whammy treatment — Canada choked to Switzerland AND Congo DR got knocked out by Colombia, so she's swinging between "maybe next round" and "actually no" territory. Ana's Qatar situation is quietly dire after that Bosnia shellacking 💀 but hey, the tournament's long and so are the odds of a Tier 3 team actually surviving.

The Underdog Hero prize is shaping up to be anyone's game at this point, but let's be real — most of you lot are about three bad results away from fighting for the Wooden Spoon instead.

Rory's Czechia got absolutely demolished 0-3 by Mexico, which is what happens when you pick a team that forgot how to play football 🫠. Meanwhile, Carole is laughing all the way to the bank with two wins including that Mexico demolition job, making the rest of us look like we threw darts at a wall. Daire's the real chaos agent though — Germany implodes 1-2 to Ecuador and suddenly he's grinning like he won the lottery, which technically he did because that's a T2 beauty.

Fiona and Ana both got the same draw with Japan-Sweden, which is either the luckiest tie-in sweepstakes history or proof they both picked the most boring result imaginable 😅. Peter's Netherlands cruised 3-1 over Tunisia while poor Lynne watched her pick get shredded, and honestly that's the difference between picking winners and picking wishes. Zach somehow scraped a 1-0 South Africa victory to stay in the Underdog Hero hunt, which tells you everything about T3 this year — survival by accident.

Derek and Ian both picked T3 teams that lost today, so they're probably eyeing that Beautiful Loser prize like it's winning the lottery. The real story though: Ronán's watching Germany get embarrassed on a day when literally anything could've happened, and instead he picked the team that didn't — that's not bad luck, that's just spectacular timing.

Mark O'Connor had the sort of day that makes the rest of us want to throw our phones in the bin 🔥. France absolutely dismantled Norway and Senegal put Iraq in the history books for all the wrong reasons, meaning Mark's racking up points like he predicted the future. Meanwhile Paul Donnelly is living the nightmare — he watched Norway get embarrassed, Iraq get obliterated, and Paraguay decided a nil-nil draw with Australia was the highlight of his day. That's rough mate.

Ian also got caught in the crossfire when Turkey decided to turn the US game into a proper Hollywood ending 😤, but at least there was drama involved. Derek's sitting pretty neutral with his Australia draw, though honestly that's the sort of result that feels like watching paint dry for ninety minutes. Zach, you sneaky bastard, Turkey actually pulling off the upset means you've got momentum while half this group is already mentally planning their semi-final funeral arrangements.

The scoreline gets uglier, the prize pool tightens up, and Paul's Iraq investment just went from "hopeful" to "possibly the worst Wooden Spoon contender we've ever seen" 💀. Mark's already spending his money in his head.

Right then, Day 17 is in the books and it's been absolutely mental 🌪️. Ana's sitting pretty with Belgium's demolition of New Zealand—five-one, honestly should be illegal—while Zach sneaks through with Spain's gritty one-nil over Uruguay. Gran's having the day of days with England's clean sheet against Panama AND that Spain result giving him a second T1 win, which means he's basically printing money at this point. Meanwhile Mark's staring down the barrel of the Wooden Spoon after New Zealand got absolutely filleted 5-1, and fair play to Carole for watching Uzbekistan get dismantled by Congo DR because that's just cruel.

The draw merchants are everywhere though—Rory's got two of them tucked away which is either genius or panic, Daire pulled an Iran stalemate, and Aidan's Portugal refusing to budge against Colombia. Sian's caught between a rock and a hard place with Egypt's Iran draw keeping her hopes alive but Ghana's loss to Croatia already looking dodgy 😬.

Ronán took it on the chin with Panama getting steamrolled by England, but honestly the real story is Fiona quietly sitting on that Congo DR upset—nobody saw the African dark horse coming and she's absolutely laughing all the way to the Underdog Hero prize 💰.

Gran's basically playing a different game at this point. The rest of yis are fighting for scraps.

Game Day 18: The Avalanche Rolls On

Sian's living her best life with Argentina demolishing Jordan 3-1, which means she's absolutely laughing into her pillow right now 🎯. Meanwhile Fiona's sneaky Canada pick just keeps printing money against South Africa, proving that sometimes the boring choice is the smart choice. Ronán somehow walked away with a 3-3 draw between Algeria and Austria, which honestly feels like winning the lottery in slow motion.

Peter got absolutely mugged today though 💔 — Austria saved him from total disaster with that draw, but his Jordan stack got obliterated by Messi's boys. And Zach? My man, your South Africa just lost 1-0 to Canada. To Canada. The nation that apologizes for winning.

With knockouts looming, Sian's cruising, Fiona's dangerous, and everyone else is white-knuckling into the next round. The beautiful thing about being bad is nobody expects anything from you anymore.

The group stage is officially done and dusted 🎉 and before we crack on with the knockout rounds, it's time to hand out two of The Avalanche's finest prizes.

💐 Beautiful Loser — €15 goes to Mark. New Zealand scored 4 goals in the group stage — the most of any team that still went home. Exciting, attacking, and ultimately not good enough. Mark collects the cash for picking the most stylish exit in the tournament.

🥄 Wooden Spoon — €5 goes to Paul. Norway conceded 7, Paraguay conceded 4, and Iraq shipped a staggering 12 goals in the group stage. That's 23 combined — a new low in Murphy/Donnelly Sweepstakes history. The fiver is yours, Paul. Wear it with pride.

As for the 🦁 Underdog Hero (€20) — still very much alive. Three Tier 3 teams are still standing: Fiona's Congo DR face England on July 1, Rory's Cape Verde take on Argentina on July 3, and Sian's Ghana face Colombia on July 4. Zach's South Africa went out quietly to Canada today and won't be troubling the leaderboard. Last one standing takes the €20 — may the best underdog win.

THE AVALANCHE - GAME DAY 19

Well well well, what a day for chaos. Daire's living his best life watching Brazil scrape past Japan while Fiona watched her T1 pick crumble 🔥 Meanwhile, Paraguay absolutely mugged Germany in the greatest heist since the Donnelly family silverware went missing, and somehow Paul is the architect of this masterclass. Four goals conceded in one match, Ronán — that's not a defence, that's a turnstile.

Paul's sitting pretty with Paraguay actually delivering like a Tier 1 team in disguise, while Daire's Brazil keeps him in the conversation. But let's be honest, watching Germany lose 5-4 is the kind of football chaos we all needed. Ronán's having a proper stinker with his T1 pick taking an absolute battering 💔

The Beautiful Loser prize is suddenly looking spicy with multiple T1 crashes incoming. Enjoy the peace while it lasts, lads — this tournament's about to eat itself alive.

The Avalanche rolls on and the casualties are piling up 🏔️ Mark's France absolutely demolished Ana's Sweden 3-0, which is what happens when you pick a Tier 2 team and they run into a buzzsaw. Meanwhile Derek's having the time of his life after Morocco's bonkers 4-3 win over Netherlands — Peter's watching his T1 pick get absolutely mugged off in what might be the wildest game of the tournament. That's what you get for being greedy with the Oranje.

Paul's basking in the glow of Norway's clinical 2-1 over Ivory Coast, while Carole's staring down the barrel of a Tier 2 exit. Derek and Paul are absolutely printing money right now with their T1 picks, but don't get too comfortable lads — the real chaos is coming 💪 Peter's got some serious work to do if Netherlands can bounce back, otherwise he's looking at Beautiful Loser money if he's lucky.

Mark's basically untouchable at this point, Ana's sweating, and Peter's learning what it means to back a Tier 1 team that plays like they've never seen a football before. The Beautiful Loser prize might be Peter's only redemption arc at this stage.

Well well well, Game Day 21 and The Avalanche continues its reign of chaos. Carole and Gran are absolutely printing money right now with back-to-back Tier 1 wins, while Ana finally got Belgium over the line in a proper thriller against Senegal 🔥 that had everyone sweating. Meanwhile Fiona's Congo DR dream got properly trampled by England's ruthless 2-1 demolition, and poor Daire watched Ecuador get absolutely schooled 0-2 by Mexico — that's what happens when you back a team playing like they forgot which sport they're in.

Mark's Senegal fought like heroes but couldn't quite close it out against Belgium in a bonkers 3-2 finish, so the losses are starting to pile up across the shop. The Tier 1 crew are absolutely feasting while our Tier 2 and 3 players are getting picked off one by one 💀 Fiona and Daire are now genuinely in the frame for Beautiful Loser money, which honestly at this point might be the most realistic path forward.

The knockout stages are looming and half this group is already playing for style points and dignity. May the odds be ever in your favour, you beautiful disasters.

What a day for the Murphy/Donnelly elite 🔥. Zach and Aidan are both grinning ear to ear after their Tier 1s absolutely demolished the opposition—Spain's 3-0 masterclass means Zach can finally stop sweating. Aidan got Portugal's gritty win over Croatia, which is the kind of narrative gold that doesn't feel cheap. Meanwhile Ian is doing what Ian does best, winning with both feet in the door, though his Croatia pick took a proper battering on the other side.

The real casualties here are Peter and Lynne, who both watched their Tier 2 picks get absolutely humbled 😬. Austria managed a respectable zero goals against Spain's relentless attack, and Bosnia just couldn't handle the American machine. Not much to salvage there except dignity and maybe a Beautiful Loser run if things get creative later. Lynne's still got knockout chances lurking, but Peter's looking increasingly like a customer for the wooden spoon rather than the €120.

Zach's got his feet up today, but don't get comfortable mate—there's still a lot of football left and someone's always one shocking result away from eating humble pie.

Right lads, Game Day 23 served up chaos and we're here for it. Lynne's sitting pretty with Switzerland steamrolling Algeria 2-0 🔥 while Sian's Argentina scraped past Cape Verde Islands 3-2 in what can only be described as the tournament's most pointless nail-biter. Poor Rory watched his Tier 3 darlings get absolutely battered by Argentina — that Cape Verde dream is well and truly dead, mate. Over in the Egypt versus Australia thriller, Derek got absolutely mugged off 😬 as Egypt somehow turned a 3-1 deficit into a 5-3 masterclass in chaos football. Ronán's Algeria joined the exit queue courtesy of Switzerland's clinical display, so that's another Tier 2 biting the dust.

Sian's walking a tightrope now with one foot in the promised land and one foot heading straight for the Beautiful Loser prize 💀 while Lynne's looking dangerously close to the serious money. Derek and Ronán can probably start mentally drafting their apology speeches, though there's still time for them to stumble upwards somehow.

The Avalanche sends its regards: Egypt just proved that scoring five goals doesn't necessarily mean you're good at football, it just means your opponent is spectacularly bad at it.

GAME DAY 24: THE AVALANCHE STRIKES

Rory's having the kind of day where picking Colombia 1-0 feels like pure genius when really it was just dumb luck 😂 Meanwhile Derek's absolutely mugging off the group with Morocco's three-goal hammering of Canada, leaving poor Fiona wondering if she accidentally picked the team that plays with eight men. Mark's quietly stacking wins like he actually knows what he's doing, but don't get cocky Mark, the tournament's got teeth yet.

Over in the chaos corner, Sian watched Ghana lose 1-0 to Colombia and is now officially fighting for Underdog Hero status with her T3 squad 🏔️ Paul's Paraguay just got a masterclass from France and the damage is done. The real question is whether Sian's survival instincts are sharp enough to keep her in the money, because that €20 is looking spicier than a Morocco shutout right now.

We're entering the business end where luck meets reckoning. Rory and Derek are flexing, Mark's circling, and Sian's basically playing 4D chess for scraps. Can't wait to see who bottlenecks next 🎯

Game Day 25: The Avalanche Strikes

Paul's living his best life right now 🔥 because Norway just absolutely mugged Brazil in a 2-1 shocker that nobody saw coming. Daire picked Brazil thinking they'd cruise through like it was a charity match, but instead he's watching Erling Haaland's cousins celebrate in the snow. This is what happens when you pick based on vibes instead of actual football, mate.

The real comedy here is that Paul's now genuinely threatening the leaderboard while Daire's sweating about his group stage credentials. Norway weren't even supposed to be this good 🇳🇴 — they're basically the Tier 1 team that showed up to Tier 1's birthday party and stayed for three rounds of drinks.

Moral of the story: never bet against a country that invented skiing and angry eyebrows.

The Avalanche rolls on and honestly it's carnage out there. Zach finally caught a break 😅 with Spain scraping past Portugal in what can only be described as a "we're just here to survive" masterclass, while Gran absolutely cleaned house watching England dismantle Mexico 3-2 in a proper thriller. Meanwhile Carole and Aidan got absolutely steamrolled on the same day, which is either terrible luck or proof the football gods have a sense of timing.

Gran's sitting pretty now with a T1 winner already locked in, which means he can watch the rest unfold like some sort of smug mastermind 🎯. Zach's also breathing easier, though let's be honest his Spain side looked about as convincing as a chocolate teapot. Carole and Aidan both need serious recovery missions because getting knocked out in the same round hurts almost as much as it hurts to watch Mexico defend like they've never seen a football before.

The beauty of it all? There's still everything to play for in the remaining brackets. Someone's gonna walk away as the Beautiful Loser, someone's getting that Underdog Hero money 💰, and someone's definitely getting that wooden spoon. All we know for sure is Zach's finally got a pulse.

Well well well, what an absolute carnival of chaos on Game Day 27. Ana's Belgium absolutely demolished Ian's United States 4-1, which is basically what happens when you let a toddler loose on the controls 🎮 Meanwhile Sian's Argentina scraped past Egypt in a thriller, banking crucial points while also sending her own Egypt squad crashing out in the second tier. That's what we call having your cake and eating it too.

The real subplot though is that bonkers Switzerland versus Colombia match where Lynne's Swiss got done 4-3 by Rory's Colombian dark horses. Yes you read that right, a 7-goal absolute belter with the "underdog" somehow surviving the group stage. Rory's grinning like he just won the lottery when technically he should be furious 😂 Meanwhile Ian's watching from the wreckage wondering if the USMNT even made it past kickoff.

Ana's on a proper run now with Belgium steamrolling through, but don't get comfortable because this sweepstakes eats cockiness for breakfast. Sian's looking sharp, Rory's riding pure luck, and honestly the rest of you better start praying your Tier 2 players show up before someone's carrying the wooden spoon home.
